subject: Project Management
title: Base Rate Thinking
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Base Rate Thinking.

hook.text: Before you estimate your project, ask: what's the base rate of success for this type of project?

buildup.text: If 70% of similar projects are late, your starting assumption should be that yours probably will be too.

discovery.text: Base rates give you reality before your optimism takes over. They're the outside view in one number.

twist.text: People ignore base rates because they feel generic. 'Our project is different.' It usually isn't.

climax.text: Using base rates as a starting point — then adjusting — produces dramatically better forecasts.

punchline.text: Start with what's typical. Then explain why you're different.

quiz.question: Why do people tend to ignore base rates?
options: They feel generic — people believe their project is different
Base rates are only available for construction projects
